data.title: Vision Marine Technologies surges on production partnership with Massimo Marine
data.description:
• Vision Marine Technologies (NASDAQ:VMAR) entered into a production partnership with Massimo Group's (NASDAQ:MAMO) marine division, Massimo Marine
  • The collaboration is to produce a fully integrated 30-foot electric pontoon platform designed for commercial and recreational markets.
  • Massimo Marine will supply pontoon boats to be equipped with Vision Marine's E-Motion 180E Powertrain System.
  • VMAR shares were up over 12% in premarket trading on Monday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Medicus Pharma collaborates to expand mid-stage cancer treatment study in Asia-Pacific region
data.description:
• Medicus Pharma (NASDAQ:MDCX) announced an agreement with Swanielle to explore expansion of Phase 2 clinical study for treatment of basal cell carcinoma in the Asia-pacific region.
  • Under the agreement, Swanielle, a healthcare consulting firm, will help identify a pharmaceutical/biotech company or companies in Southeast Asia to partner with Medicus to expand the mid-stage study in the Asia Pacific region.
  • The company also said that the study is currently underway in nine clinical sites in the United States and has already randomized more than 25% of the 60 patients expected to be enrolled in the study.
  • Medicus is planning to initiate an interim data analysis in Q1 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nu Holdings cut to Sell at Citi as growth trajectory challenged
data.description:
Citi downgraded Nu Holdings (NYSE:NU), which operates NuBank in Brazil and Mexico, to Sell from Neutral as it faces a tougher road for growth after rapid expansion.

Nu Holdings (NYSE:NU) stock dipped 2.2% in Monday premarket trading.

Q3 2024 saw a combination of lower average revenue per active customer Q/Q, higher cost of funding, and Q/Q decline in net interest income, mainly in credit cards, analyst Gustavo Schroden wrote in a note to clients.

In addition, risk-adjusted net interest margin fell 90 basis points Q/Q. "As a result, its pretax earnings remain virtually stable Q/Q after nine consecutive quarters of expansion," Schroden said.

He expects it will take longer than expected for alternative revenue sources, such as payroll loans in Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia, to offset the slowdown in credit cards and personal loans in Brazil.

Meanwhile, Citi reiterates its Buy rating for Inter & Co. (INTR), noting that the company is still in the early stage of growth in unsecured loans and its diversification efforts could support even faster growth in NII/NIM.

Schroden's Sell rating is even more bearish than the SA Quant rating of Hold and clashes with the average SA Analyst and average Wall Street ratings, both at Buy.

data.title: Intel announces CEO Gelsinger retiring; replaced with co-CEOs (update)
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) made the surprising announcement on Monday that Chief Executive Officer Pat Gelsinger is retiring and will be replaced by co-CEOs David Zinsner and Michelle Johnston Holthaus.

Gelsinger is also stepping down from the company's board of directors. Both departures are effective December 1.

Holthaus has been appointed to the newly created position of CEO of Intel Products, while Frank Yeary was named interim executive chair.

“On behalf of the board, I want to thank Pat for his many years of service and dedication to Intel across a long career in technology leadership,” Yeary said in a statement. “Pat spent his formative years at Intel, then returned at a critical time for the company in 2021. As a leader, Pat helped launch and revitalize process manufacturing by investing in state-of-the-art semiconductor manufacturing, while working tirelessly to drive innovation throughout the company.”

“While we have made significant progress in regaining manufacturing competitiveness and building the capabilities to be a world-class foundry, we know that we have much more work to do at the company and are committed to restoring investor confidence,” Yeary continued. “As a board, we know first and foremost that we must put our product group at the center of all we do. Our customers demand this from us, and we will deliver for them. With MJ’s permanent elevation to CEO of Intel Products along with her interim co-CEO role of Intel, we are ensuring the product group will have the resources needed to deliver for our customers. Ultimately, returning to process leadership is central to product leadership, and we will remain focused on that mission while driving greater efficiency and improved profitability.”

Shares rose 4% in premarket trading on the back of the announcement.

Zinsner joined Intel in 2022 from Micron (MU), where he was executive vice president and CFO. He has also worked in leadership roles at other companies, including being president and chief operating officer at Affirmed Networks and senior vice president of finance and CFO at Analog Devices (ADI).

Holthaus was previously executive vice president and general manager of Intel's Client Computing Group and has had a number of other leadership roles at the company, including chief revenue officer and general manager of the Sales and Marketing Group, and lead of global CCG sales.

Santa Clara, Calif.-based Intel has come under intense scrutiny in recent memory as the company struggles to keep up with competitors AMD (AMD) and Nvidia (NVDA) in the artificial intelligence space and has had several hiccups with its nascent foundry business, designed to compete with Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM).

Intel announced in August that it was letting go 15% of its staff and cut its spending and headcount by $10B as it works to streamline its operations.

Separately in August, Lip-Bu Tan left Intel's board of directors, with speculation that he departed amid concerns about the company's plan to turn itself around.

In the most recently reported quarter, Intel said it lost $0.46 per share as revenue fell 6.2% year-over-year to come in at $13.28B. Included in that was an 8% decline in foundry revenue to $4.4B.

However, the company said it made "significant progress" on its $10B cost reduction plan, as it took a $2.8B restructuring charge during the period.

At the time, Gelsinger said he intended to keep the company together, amid speculation that the company would spin off its foundry unit.

Last week, the Biden administration finalized a $7.86B award to Intel from the CHIPs Act to boost U.S.-based manufacturing, down from a previously announced $8.5B.

This is breaking news...

data.title: Zebra upgraded on improving market demand: Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Zebra Technologies (NASDAQ:ZBRA) was upgraded to Equal-Weight from Underweight by Morgan Stanley due to improving market demand over the next twelve months and a growing customer base.

The price target was also increased by more than 30% to $400 from $305. Zebra shares have steadily climbed throughout the year, gaining more than 70% over the past 12 months. Morgan Stanley finds that Zebra's current valuation of $407 is slightly ahead of the recovery.

Zebra is a mobile computing company based in Lincolnshire, Ill., a northern suburb of Chicago.

"With our latest refresh of our retail/distribution capex tracker improving and consumer survey data points turning more positive, we no longer see a negative catalyst and view next twelve months risk-reward as more balanced," said Morgan Stanley analysts Meta Marshall and Karan Juvekar. "In order to get more positive, we would like to see ZBRA approach high single-digit growth rates (i.e. outperform 5-7% long-term targets), which requires inflection in non-core segments like RFID or machine vision, not contemplated in our base case."

Marshall also noted that Zebra was seeing large North American projects increase during the third quarter, which are expected to continue through the fourth quarter as well.

"We believe there is potential for a meaningful refresh cycle in 2025 for customers that built out capacity during the pandemic, as typical mobile computer refresh cycles are 4-5 years in nature, though we are still cautious on degree of opportunity," he added.

Seeking Alpha analyst Hunter Wolf Research recently gave Zebra a Buy rating and set a $450 12-month price target on the stock.

Zebra management guides for 28% to 31% revenue growth during the fourth quarter and projects $850M in free cash flow for fiscal year 2024, which highlights strong end-market recovery.

However, Hunter Wolf noted nearly 50% of finished goods production is based in China, and if tariffs are installed, it could affect margins in the near term.

data.title: U.S. Physical Therapy buys eight-clinic hand and physical therapy practice
data.description:
• U.S. Physical Therapy (NYSE:USPH) has acquired a 75% stake in a hand and physical therapy practice with eight clinic locations in Wyoming.
  • The current owners retain a 25% ownership interest. The practice currently generates approximately $6.5M in annual revenues and 43,000 in annual visits.

data.title: Novocure therapy succeeds in pancreatic cancer trial
data.description:
Novocure (NASDAQ:NVCR) has met the primary endpoint of overall survival in a late-stage trial evaluating the use of its Tumor Treating Fields (TTFields) therapy for pancreatic cancer.

Shares of the oncology company jumped 25% premarket on Monday.

The pivotal, Phase 3 PANOVA-3 trial met its primary endpoint, demonstrating a statistically significant improvement in median overall survival versus control.

The study is designed to test the efficacy and safety of TTFields therapy used concomitantly with gemcitabine and nab-paclitaxel, as a first-line treatment of locally advanced pancreatic adenocarcinoma.

Patients were randomized to receive either TTFields therapy concomitant with gemcitabine and nab-paclitaxel or gemcitabine and nab-paclitaxel alone.

Novocure (NVCR) plans to file for regulatory approval of TTFields in unresectable, locally advanced pancreatic adenocarcinoma based on PANOVA-3.

The company is also exploring the use of TTFields therapy together with atezolizumab, gemcitabine and nab-paclitaxel for the treatment of metastatic pancreatic cancer in a Phase 2 PANOVA-4 trial, with data anticipated in 2026.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson seeks approval of pediatric indications for TREMFYA
data.description:
• Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) submitted two supplemental Biologics License Applications to the U.S. FDA seeking approval of TREMFYA for treating children 6 years and older with moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis (PsO) and children 5 years of age and older with active juvenile psoriatic arthritis.
  • The submission is based on data from the late stage study in pediatric patients with moderate to severe plaque PsO and bridging pharmacokinetic data from the Phase 3 VOYAGE 1 and 2 studies.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bausch Health names Jonathan Sadeh as chief medical officer
data.description:
• Bausch Health Companies (NYSE:BHC) said it has appointed Jonathan Sadeh as its chief medical officer.
  • Sadeh has over 20 years of expertise, and most recently at Bristol-Meyers Squibb, he was responsible for multiple drug approvals in dermatology and gastroenterology.

data.title: MicroStrategy bought ~$1.5B in bitcoin from Nov. 25-Dec. 1
data.description:
• MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) purchased 15,400 bitcoins for about $1.5B in cash during the period between Nov. 25 and Dec. 1, the company said in a filing.
  • The purchases were made using proceeds from the company’s recent $21B stock offering.
  • As of Dec. 1, approximately $11.3B of shares remained available for sale pursuant to the sales deal.
  • The company held an aggregate of about 402.1K bitcoin as of Dec. 1, which were acquired at an aggregate purchase price of around $23.4B.
